overview: Boros Dragons is a white-red aggressive deck that leverages low-cost flying creatures and combat tricks to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize. The strategy revolves around applying immediate aerial pressure while holding instant-speed buffs to bypass blockers or push lethal damage. By maintaining a fast curve and utilizing high-impact finishers, the deck aims to close out games in the early to mid stages of Standard.
win_conditions: Flying combat pressure with low-cost dragons
Overwhelm with high-toughness flyers like Nova Hellkite

keep_criteria: Two-land hands with a 1 or 2-drop dragon
Hands with Voice of Victory and early attackers
Hands that can play a dragon on turn 2
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (Plains, Mountain, or shock lands) and at least one 1- or 2-dropped threat or mana accelerator.
Prioritize hands containing {W} or {R} sources that enable casting Voice of Victory, Clarion Conqueror, or Nova Hellkite by turn 3.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with three or more lands
Hands with only late-game cards like Nova Hellkite
Mulligan any hand with 3+ lands, no playable first turn, or only 3-drops without land support.

early_game: Play lands and low-cost flyers immediately
Use combat tricks to swing for damage
Apply pressure to force blockers or win quickly
Turn 1: Play a land, ideally a shock land or fetch/shock combo to ensure color consistency.
Turn 2: Cast a cheap dragon or mana accelerant, then play a second land.
Turn 3: Drop a signature dragon or chain into a second threat.
If holding multiple dragons, sequence cheaper ones first to establish board presence, then swing with haste.
Always ensure you have a land in hand before committing to the board.
mid_game: Use Voice of Victory to push lethal or clear blockers
Maintain board presence with flying threats
Preserve mana for instant-speed responses (Voice of Victory's ability, Nova's sacrifice trigger) while maintaining board presence.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without protection if facing control.
Use Clarion Conqueror's token generation to fuel sacrifice outlets or block efficiently.
Track graveyard activity if facing discard or graveyard hate.
Commit threats when you have priority to resolve them or when the opponent has no removal.
Hold back a dragon if you suspect a sweep or targeted removal.
late_game: Deploy Nova Hellkite as a board wipe and finisher
Use combat tricks to bypass removal
Close out games with unblockable flying damage
Nova Hellkite with sacrifice outlets for massive trample swings, or Clarion Conqueror flooding the board with flying tokens.
Voice of Victory can clear the path by removing key blockers or control permanents.
Combine with direct damage or pump spells to close out the game.
Go all-in when you have board advantage, the opponent is tapped out, or you've removed their sweepers/removal.
Commit when you can swing through their blockers or when they have no answers to flying threats.
Use Nova's sacrifice ability to bypass blockers or finish the game in a single combat phase.

key_cards.name: Voice of Victory
key_cards.notes: Hold up for blockers or swing for lethal; prioritize giving +2/+2 to creatures that can deal immediate damage.

key_cards.name: Clarion Conqueror
key_cards.notes: Play on turn 2 to establish air superiority; use its flying to bypass ground blockers and pressure life total.

key_cards.name: Nova Hellkite
key_cards.notes: Deploy when opponent has removal or board presence; use its enter-the-battlefield effect to clear threats and swing for lethal.

sequencing_tips: Hold Voice of Victory until opponent commits to removal or blockers
Play Clarion Conqueror before casting instants to maximize early pressure
Save Nova Hellkite for when opponent has multiple threats or board control spells
Common mistake: Overcommitting to the board without considering sweepers or targeted removal.
Always keep a land in hand if possible.
Nova Hellkite's sacrifice ability is powerful but can backfire if you have no targets
plan your turns to have a creature ready to sacrifice.
Voice of Victory's ability is instant speed—use it to remove blockers before combat or answer threats.
Clarion Conqueror's tokens are great for blocking and fueling sacrifice outlets.

matchups.advice: Push early damage before they stabilize with countermagic and board wipes.
Use flying to bypass ground creatures and hold combat tricks for their big threats.
Race them by pushing damage through flying threats before they stabilize with removal.

Use cheap threats, split your casting across turns, employ discard to remove counterspells, or use instant-speed threats. If possible, bring in cards that cannot be countered or have protection. Force them to use multiple counterspells by threatening multiple paths to victory.

Apply early pressure to beat their ramp curve. Use targeted removal for their mana dorks. Do not trade inefficiently; use flying to bypass early ground threats. Force them to use removal on your threats while you stabilize and close the game before their late-game monsters come online.

Use Voice of Victory to remove key early threats. Hold back a dragon for a counter-swing. Use Clarion tokens to block efficiently. Do not overextend into combat tricks or pump spells. Prioritize life total and board presence over greedy plays.

Pressure consistently, force them to use removal on multiple threats. Use discard to strip key answers. Swing with flying threats they cannot block. Force them to play around your threats while you stabilize. Use Nova's sacrifice ability to remove their key permanents or clear the way for combat.

Win the resource war. Prioritize keeping lands and cheap threats. Use discard to strip their key dragons. Trade efficiently, favoring trades where you get value (e.g., Clarion tokens versus their dragons). Control the board with Voice of Victory's removal ability. Outlast them in the late game by maintaining card advantage and board presence.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Izzet decks), ramp dorks (Landfall), and token generators (Spellementals).
Note when they tap out for spells versus creatures.
Pay attention to graveyard activity and whether they are holding up mana for sweepers or targeted removal.
Track their life total to calculate when you can swing for lethal.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Voice of Victory, Clarion Conqueror, Nova Hellkite

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Voice of Victory, Clarion Conqueror, Nova Hellkite
